conditions.message: Water loss outruns root supply → edge burn, slowed growth. Pull: shrink VPD (raise RH / drop leaf temp) and tighten irrigation during peak.
Stomata clamp shut; photosynthesis and Ca transport drop. Pull: raise RH or lower temp, and ease drybacks (more frequent pulses).
Cumulative light dose exceeds carbon capacity → gradual bleach/necrosis. Pull: reduce DLI/peak PPFD or add CO₂ + keep leaf temp in range.
You’re light-saturated but carbon-limited → wasted photons. Pull: raise CO₂ with good mixing or lower PPFD.

conditions.condition: Rhizosphere acid stress → nutrient availability shift
conditions.message: Acidic root zone locks out Ca/Mg/K and irritates roots. Pull: raise feed pH gradually and add leach to reset.
Wrong strength drives either burn or starvation and unstable uptake. Pull: match recipe EC, recalibrate meter, and stabilize reservoir.
Off-target pH makes nutrients unavailable (fake deficiencies). Pull: adjust feed to media range and confirm with runoff after 1–2 irrigations.
Warm solution holds less O₂ → root stress and pathogen risk. Pull: cool feed/reservoir to 18–22°C and insulate.
Salts stack and burn root tips; uptake stalls. Pull: increase runoff/leach and tighten cadence until PWEC≈Bulk.
Media dries too far → EC spikes, wilt, stalled Ca flow. Pull: increase frequency/shot size and reduce dryback target.
Roots suffocate → droop, slow growth, disease pressure. Pull: increase dryback between events, improve aeration/drainage, reduce saturation.
Transpiration is too low → sluggish uptake and soft tissue. Pull: raise VPD (dehumidify or raise temp) and add gentle airflow.
Stretch and weak biomass → low trajectory. Pull: raise PPFD to stage and keep VPD/CO₂ matched.
Slow growth compounds over time → airy structure and missed yield. Pull: raise DLI consistently (not spikes) and support with CO₂/nutrition.
Energy transfer bottleneck → purpling and slowed growth. Pull: keep root temps up, hit pH range, and ensure balanced P (no PK hammering).
PM steals photosynthesis and spreads fast. Pull: lower RH/raise VPD, increase airflow, and run a PM-safe control rotation.
Grey mold rots dense sites—damage is permanent. Pull: prevent condensation (lower RH, dehumidify into lights-off), increase airflow, thin dense areas.
Sap loss + stress → speckle, curl, stalled growth; explodes in hot-dry. Pull: isolate, implement rotation (biocontrol/miticide), and avoid hot-dry peaks.
Uniform paling and slowed growth; yield potential drops. Pull: confirm pH/EC first, then raise N slightly within target EC.
Interveinal chlorosis reduces photosynthesis. Pull: correct pH then add modest Mg (e.g., Epsom 0.25–0.5 g/L for 1–2 feeds).
K crowds out Ca/Mg → crispy edges and stalled new growth. Pull: lower K/overall EC, add leach until PWEC≈Bulk, pause PK spikes.
New growth yellows while veins stay greener; growth stalls. Pull: lower root-zone pH, address alkalinity, add chelated Fe, and keep top PPFD modest until corrected.
High EC blocks water uptake → tip burn, droop, slow growth. Pull: gentle flush/leach to bring PWEC near Bulk, then resume 10–20% lower EC.
New growth distorts/tips burn because Ca can’t reach fast tissues. Pull: stabilize VPD (avoid extremes), add peak pulses (Ca rides transpiration), ensure Ca supply and healthy roots.
Photosystems overload → taco/bleach and stalled growth. Pull: reduce peak PPFD, cool leaf temps, improve airflow/uniformity.
High pH locks out Fe/Mn/Mg → chlorosis that won’t fix with more feed. Pull: lower feed pH gradually, reduce alkalinity, recheck runoff trend.

conditions.condition: Excess N → vegetative bias + osmotic imbalance
conditions.message: Too much N → dark claw, soft growth, delayed finish. Pull: reduce N/EC 10–20%, increase runoff briefly, keep PPFD/VPD sane.
Wrong photoperiod triggers early flower or stalls veg. Pull: keep veg hours stable (e.g., 18h) and control DLI via PPFD, not hours.
Light leaks/extra hours cause foxtails and re-veg traits. Pull: enforce 12h dark strict and eliminate leaks.
Light exceeds carbon supply → wasted watts and less yield. Pull: raise CO₂ (with mixing) or reduce PPFD; keep VPD steady.
High CO₂ at low light makes soft, stretchy growth and wastes gas. Pull: lower CO₂ setpoint or raise PPFD; keep VPD moderate.
Root-zone EC stratifies; hot pockets burn roots. Pull: increase leach/runoff and improve uniform wetting until PWEC≈Bulk.
Excess runoff washes ions and swings pH → drifting deficiencies. Pull: cut leach %, stabilize feed pH/alkalinity, raise EC modestly if needed.
EC creeps up day after day → burn and stalled uptake. Pull: lower feed EC and add 10–20% runoff until PWEC converges.
Media gets depleted → pale growth and weak stems. Pull: raise EC slightly and reduce excessive runoff; confirm on new growth.
Post-flush plants can look hungry and unstable. Pull: return to balanced feed (slightly lower EC), keep pH steady, and monitor PWEC recovery.
Runoff lies because water bypasses media; salts hide in pockets. Pull: fix emitter coverage, saturate fully, and sample composite runoff.
High alkalinity burns buffer and pushes pH up → micro lockout. Pull: RO blend/pretreat, control alkalinity, and keep feed pH consistent.
Too much NH₄ drives pH down and skews cations. Pull: shift toward nitrate‑N, raise pH slightly, and monitor new growth.
Low oxygen roots invite rot → chronic droop and stalled uptake. Pull: reduce saturation, increase aeration/drainage, sanitize, manage root temps.

conditions.condition: Ca mass-flow failure from water stress
conditions.message: Deep drybacks choke Ca flow → tip burn and brittle growth. Pull: shorten intervals/add peak pulses and avoid extreme VPD peaks.
Demand > supply → margins burn even with ‘good’ feed. Pull: lower VPD/PPFD peaks and tighten irrigation during peak demand.
Closed stomata slow growth and raise in-canopy humidity → disease risk. Pull: bring VPD into band, stabilize airflow, avoid hard drybacks.
Peak PPFD is too spiky → tops burn while DLI looks fine. Pull: lower peak or raise fixtures; spread light over longer window.
Too low/flat peak gives stretch and airy structure. Pull: increase peak PPFD or improve uniformity while keeping total DLI sensible.
Plants aren’t actually taking up what you’re feeding. Pull: inspect roots, ensure full wetting/drain, and correct irrigation uniformity.
Long gaps during peak demand cause wilt then salt spikes. Pull: add mid‑peak pulses and shorten P1/P2 intervals.
Hot leaves + low CO₂ wastes energy instead of building biomass. Pull: lower leaf temp or raise CO₂; keep VPD stable.
Stagnant humid pockets waste CO₂ and favor pathogens. Pull: improve mixing/air exchange and control RH (especially lights‑off).
High light drives N demand; canopy will pale uniformly. Pull: increase N slightly (within EC target) or reduce DLI until balanced.
Too much N biases lush, weak growth and clawing. Pull: cut N 10–20% and keep PPFD/VPD appropriate.
Salts are accumulating quietly; the spike is coming. Pull: introduce runoff now (10–20%) and tighten cadence.
Big wet‑dry swings shred fine roots and slow uptake. Pull: tighten VWC band with smaller, more frequent events.
Constant wet prevents root renewal and invites hypoxia. Pull: increase dryback targets and improve aeration/drainage.
Current drivers will push extra stretch after flip. Pull: manage VPD/PPFD early post‑flip and get support/trellis installed before stretch peaks.
VPD swings yo‑yo stomata → stress and inconsistent uptake. Pull: tune controller gains and smooth lights-on/off transitions.
Top layer is overexposed → bleaching/necrosis. Pull: raise/dim fixtures, improve uniformity, and cool leaf temps.
Cold slows metabolism and nutrient uptake → purple, droop, stalled growth. Pull: raise canopy/root temps and avoid big night drops.
Your RH/VPD pattern favors PM. Pull: dehumidify, keep VPD above threshold, increase airflow, avoid leaf wetness.
CO₂ is above what light can use → waste and soft stretch. Pull: lower CO₂ setpoint or raise PPFD; keep VPD steady.
Environment is primed for bud rot in dense sites. Pull: lower RH, dehumidify into lights‑off, increase airflow through canopy, thin hotspots.
Symptoms persist because nutrients aren’t available, not because they’re missing. Pull: bring feed/runoff pH into range gradually and stabilize alkalinity.
Roots are oxygen-starved and disease-prone → chronic droop. Pull: increase dryback/aeration, sanitize, manage temps, avoid warm saturation.
Cation ratios drift → edge burn, distortion, weak new growth. Pull: rebalance Ca:Mg, moderate PK/K spikes, keep pH stable.
High K blocks Mg → edge burn + interveinal chlorosis. Pull: lower K/EC, add modest Mg, and reset PWEC≈Bulk.
Gaps are too long → deep dryback and salt concentration. Pull: shorten intervals and add mid‑peak pulses.
Shots don’t wet the root zone evenly → dry pockets and salt stacking. Pull: increase shot volume to full saturation with 10–15% runoff.
High demand + deep dryback starves Ca to tips. Pull: add peak pulses, reduce dryback target, and moderate VPD.
Leaves desiccate fast → wilt and scorch. Pull: drop leaf temp 2–3°C, raise RH, and irrigate immediately.
One zone is getting cooked; averages are lying. Pull: flatten the intensity map (raise/dim/re-space fixtures) and cool tops.
High PPFD is capped by CO₂ availability. Pull: raise CO₂ with proper mixing or reduce PPFD peak.
CO₂ is overfed for the light level. Pull: reduce CO₂ or raise PPFD; keep VPD stable.
Peak is too high even if DLI is okay. Pull: lower peak PPFD and spread the DLI across the day.
Leaf temp runs hot → stress and stalled uptake. Pull: reduce radiant load (raise/dim), increase airflow, improve cooling.
Cold + humid stalls transpiration → nutrient uptake drags. Pull: warm canopy/roots and raise VPD slightly.
Dew forms and disease follows. Pull: dehumidify into lights‑off, keep leaf temps above dewpoint, maintain gentle airflow.
Local RH pockets + low airflow let PM explode. Pull: increase mixing/defoliate dense zones and keep VPD above threshold.
Dense, stagnant humidity rots from inside out. Pull: lower RH, increase canopy airflow, thin dense sites, avoid condensation.
Rapid VPD swings stress stomata and uptake. Pull: tune control gains and buffer with steady airflow/humidity.
Wind + dry air strips the boundary layer → edge scorch. Pull: reduce fan intensity or lower VPD (raise RH).
Far‑red bias drives internode stretch. Pull: reduce far‑red, increase main PPFD, keep VPD moderate.
UV dose is damaging tissue, especially when hot. Pull: reduce UV exposure and keep leaf temps down.
Daylength is pushing flowering signals in veg. Pull: keep veg photoperiod stable; control DLI with PPFD, not hours.
Light leaks or extended hours trigger foxtails/re‑veg traits. Pull: enforce strict 12h darkness and eliminate leaks.
Peak transpiration outpaces water supply → wilt and EC spikes. Pull: add mid‑peak pulses and shorten intervals during peak light.
No runoff for days = hidden salt stack + shrinking roots. Pull: rebuild schedule to full wetting + periodic runoff; confirm with composite runoff.
Media stays too wet → root oxygen debt. Pull: increase dryback between events and improve drainage/aeration.
Under high demand, small shots can’t keep up → dry pockets and spikes. Pull: increase shot size and/or frequency during peak.
Shots are so big they bypass media and dilute runoff readings. Pull: shrink shot size, keep frequency, avoid bypass drain.
Dry media + dry air stacks stress fast. Pull: lower VPD and add pulses; avoid deep drybacks.
Wet media + humid air = hypoxia and disease pressure. Pull: raise VPD and allow more dryback between events.
Not enough leach to control rising EC. Pull: increase runoff % and lower feed EC until PWEC converges.
Salt is building unseen; expect a jump. Pull: add controlled leach now and tighten cadence.
Hot pockets are much saltier than the average. Pull: increase leach and improve uniform wetting until PWEC drops toward Bulk.
Runoff is diluted by channeling; media may still be salty. Pull: fix distribution, pulse to full wetting, and take composite samples.
Nutrients wash out faster than plants can use them. Pull: reduce leach %, stabilize pH, and restore EC gradually.
Salts stay high because roots aren’t drinking. Pull: lower EC, increase leach, and check root oxygen/temperature.
Media is washing out; deficiencies are next. Pull: cut excessive runoff and bring feed EC back to target.
Micros become unavailable → new growth chlorosis. Pull: lower pH/alkalinity and keep media in range.
Key cations get locked out → burn and weak structure. Pull: raise pH gradually and avoid ammonium-heavy feeds.
Bicarbonates push pH up over time. Pull: RO blend/pretreat and manage alkalinity before chasing pH.
NH₄ drives pH down and destabilizes Ca/Mg/K uptake. Pull: reduce NH₄ fraction, shift to nitrate, raise pH slightly.

conditions.condition: pH-induced nutrient dysfunction with visible symptoms
conditions.message: Until pH stops drifting, symptoms won’t resolve. Pull: fix source alkalinity, stabilize feed pH, and recheck runoff trend.
Cation imbalance shows as margin burn/distortion. Pull: rebalance Ca:Mg, moderate K spikes, keep VPD and pH stable.
Excess K blocks Mg uptake. Pull: lower K/EC, add modest Mg, and reset root-zone EC.
Too much N with too little light = dark claw and stretch. Pull: reduce N/EC or raise PPFD; keep VPD moderate.

conditions.condition: N limitation under high photon flux
conditions.message: High light/fast growth outstrips N supply → uniform chlorosis. Pull: raise N slightly (within EC target) and ensure pH is in range.
Na/Cl accumulate, burn tips, and block uptake. Pull: audit water source, RO blend, and increase leach to purge.
Feed isn’t driving media toward target; uptake stays unstable. Pull: adjust feed EC/pH and irrigation to drive PWEC≈Bulk.
Warm + wet roots lose oxygen fast. Pull: cool root zone, improve aeration, reduce saturation.
Cold roots stop drinking → deficiencies show. Pull: warm feed/root zone to 18–22°C and insulate pots/lines.

conditions.condition: Warm feed → dissolved O₂ ↓ + root respiration ↑
conditions.message: Hot nutrient solution stresses roots and reduces dissolved oxygen. Pull: cool reservoir/lines and avoid heat soak.
Plant will wilt fast and damage can happen same day. Pull: irrigate now and drop VPD (raise RH / lower temp).
Salt + drought creates osmotic lock → wilt despite irrigation. Pull: gentle flush to drop EC, then tighten cadence.
Demand exceeds root supply → margin burn. Pull: lower VPD/PPFD peaks and increase peak irrigation frequency.
Closed stomata throttles CO₂ and raises disease risk. Pull: bring VPD/leaf temp into band and remove the stress driver.
Still air depletes CO₂ at the leaf surface. Pull: add canopy mixing/airflow and verify distribution.
CO₂ is present but stomata aren’t pulling. Pull: raise VPD slightly or lower CO₂ setpoint.
Dry air closes stomata while CO₂ is already low. Pull: lower VPD and/or raise CO₂; stabilize irrigation.

conditions.condition: Dynamic stress: fluctuating light + stomatal lag
conditions.message: Rapid light + VPD swings cause stomatal whiplash. Pull: smooth dimming curves and tune humidity/temperature control.
Hotspots burn tops even when averages look safe. Pull: flatten intensity map and improve air mixing at canopy.
Overnight VPD dries media and leaf tissue too hard. Pull: lower night VPD and consider a pre‑lights‑off pulse.
Stale pockets become CO₂-poor and pathogen-friendly. Pull: increase circulation, thin dense sites, and improve air exchange.
Low airflow keeps buds wet → rot risk. Pull: increase airflow through canopy and keep RH controlled, especially at night.
O₂-starved roots invite pathogens and chronic droop. Pull: increase dryback/aeration, sanitize, and manage temps.
Hot-dry stress primes pest explosions (mites) and weakens defenses. Pull: moderate VPD/leaf temp and run consistent IPM.
High EC + low runoff during peak demand burns roots fast. Pull: lower feed EC and add 10–20% runoff during peak window.
Underlit plants stretch and build weak structure. Pull: raise PPFD/uniformity and keep VPD moderate to control internodes.
Not enough photons day after day → slow biomass accumulation. Pull: increase DLI to stage target (gradually) and support with CO₂.
Daily light load is too high for carbon processing → cumulative burn. Pull: reduce DLI/peaks or raise CO₂ and manage leaf temp.
Peak is too low/flat → airy structure even if DLI adds up. Pull: increase peak PPFD or improve distribution.
Some zones are dry/salty while others drain—bad data and worse roots. Pull: audit emitters, ensure full wetting, and sample composite runoff.
Skipping irrigations during high demand causes crashes. Pull: add alarms and schedule pulses in the peak light/VPD window.
Long gaps concentrate salts in the root zone. Pull: shorten intervals and include periodic runoff to control EC.
Too-frequent leaching strips media and drives deficiencies. Pull: lengthen intervals or reduce runoff; restore EC to target.

conditions.condition: CO₂ delivery limited by poor mixing
conditions.message: High CO₂ without mixing creates dead zones and waste. Pull: fix circulation/air exchange before raising CO₂ setpoint.
Triple stress fries leaf margins. Pull: lower EC, reduce VPD peaks, and tighten irrigation during peak.

conditions.condition: Nutrient supply limitation under high carbon demand
conditions.message: High DLI demands more nutrition; canopy pales. Pull: raise EC/N slightly (or reduce DLI temporarily) and confirm pH.
VWC stays high and flat → hypoxic roots and slow uptake. Pull: increase dryback and reduce frequency/shot size.
Large swings damage fine roots and slow recovery. Pull: stabilize VWC with smaller, more frequent pulses.
Taco leaves are often heat/light, not N toxicity. Pull: check leaf temp/PPFD and airflow before cutting N.
Big DLI jump shocks plants at flip. Pull: ramp PPFD/DLI gradually while keeping VPD/CO₂ aligned.
Cold air + roots slow uptake and metabolism across the board. Pull: raise canopy/root temps 2–3°C and reduce night drops.

conditions.condition: High vapor deficit → xylem tension/cavitation risk
conditions.message: Big dewpoint gap dries tissue fast and closes stomata. Pull: raise RH or lower temp to shrink the gap; keep gentle airflow.
VPD spikes create burn and inconsistent uptake. Pull: tame controller gains and buffer with steady airflow/humidification.
Too soft (low VPD / low light) builds weak tissue and stretch. Pull: raise VPD slightly and bring PPFD/DLI up to stage.
Local PPFD/heat peaks bleach tops first. Pull: flatten intensity map (raise/dim/spread fixtures) and improve cooling/mixing.
Erratic photoperiod/dimming confuses plant signaling. Pull: keep photoperiod strict and smooth dimming curves.
pH is drifting up; micro lockouts are next. Pull: feed slightly lower pH, check alkalinity, and monitor runoff trend.
Poor uptake leaves salts behind → rising PWEC. Pull: add a bit more leach and lower feed EC until uptake recovers.
Drybacks concentrate EC and stress roots. Pull: reduce dryback amplitude and add a mid‑peak pulse.
High alkalinity/Na/Cl drives pH drift and toxicity. Pull: RO blend/pretreat and monitor source alkalinity/EC.

conditions.condition: Combined saturation + low O₂ → root respiration failure
conditions.message: Wet media + low oxygen stalls roots and invites disease. Pull: increase dryback/aeration and manage root temps.
High light with low transpiration increases Ca issues and soft tissue. Pull: raise VPD modestly and ensure Ca supply + peak pulses.
Too many events keep the root zone saturated. Pull: lengthen intervals and hit your dryback target.
Shots are too big → bypass drain and unstable EC. Pull: reduce shot size and maintain frequency; target clean runoff %.
CO₂ setpoint is high but distribution is poor. Pull: increase mixing/air exchange before raising CO₂.

conditions.condition: Cold feed shock → root membrane transport inhibition
conditions.message: Cold feed chills roots and stalls uptake. Pull: warm reservoir/lines to 18–22°C and insulate.
Excess leach washes nutrients out and destabilizes pH. Pull: reduce runoff % and restore EC to target.
Overnight depletion stresses roots and spikes EC at lights-on. Pull: lower night VPD and/or add a pre‑lights‑off pulse.
Rapid dehydration → wilt and edge scorch. Pull: drop leaf temp, raise RH, and irrigate immediately.
High PPFD + hot leaves cooks tops. Pull: reduce peak PPFD, cool leaves, and improve airflow/uniformity.
Hotspot + high VPD bleaches new growth fast. Pull: flatten light map and bring VPD to mid‑band.
Photons exceed CO₂ supply → wasted light and stalled growth. Pull: raise CO₂ with mixing or dim PPFD.
CO₂ pools and pathogens love the stagnant air. Pull: increase mixing/air exchange and align CO₂ to PPFD.
Condensation at lights-off kickstarts PM/Botrytis. Pull: dehumidify before/during lights-off and keep leaf temps above dewpoint.
Environment is in PM’s comfort zone. Pull: raise VPD, increase airflow, and run PM controls.
Humidity + density primes grey mold. Pull: lower RH, thin dense sites, increase airflow, prevent condensation.
Temp/RH swings stress stomata and uptake. Pull: tune controller gains and smooth transitions.

conditions.condition: Spatial transpiration mismatch → root-zone moisture swings
conditions.message: Uneven light/heat causes uneven VWC and EC. Pull: improve uniformity and zone irrigation if needed.

conditions.condition: Stomatal closure → carbon starvation under dehydration
conditions.message: Dry air closes stomata while CO₂ is low → carbon crash. Pull: lower VPD and raise CO₂ (or dim PPFD).
Low VPD keeps stomata lazy → CO₂ wasted. Pull: raise VPD slightly or lower CO₂ setpoint.
Low PPFD makes stretch unavoidable. Pull: raise PPFD/DLI and keep VPD moderate to tighten internodes.
UV + hot leaves bleaches tissue. Pull: reduce UV dose and lower leaf temps/PPFD peaks.
Far‑red bias amplifies stretch. Pull: reduce far‑red and increase main PPFD/VPD balance.
Stale humid air = disease + poor CO₂ use. Pull: increase mixing, thin canopy, and dehumidify.

conditions.condition: Forced desiccation injury at leaf margins
conditions.message: Excess fan + dry air strips edges. Pull: reduce fan intensity and/or raise RH (lower VPD).

conditions.condition: Photoperiod signaling disruption under high energy load
conditions.message: Photoperiod/DLI changes are too aggressive → stress/foxtails. Pull: correct photoperiod and ramp PPFD gradually.
Hot bright hotspots bleach first when CO₂ is limiting. Pull: fix mixing/CO₂ distribution and reduce hotspots.
Top canopy dries and burns. Pull: cool tops (raise/dim) and bring VPD into band.

conditions.condition: Extreme vapor deficit → cavitation/leaf desiccation risk
conditions.message: Multiple drivers push dehydration beyond uptake capacity. Pull: lower VPD, tighten irrigation cadence, and watch leaf temps.
Stomata clamp; carbon + Ca transport crash. Pull: raise RH/lower temp, raise CO₂ (with mixing), and avoid deep drybacks.
Warm, wet roots lose oxygen fast → droop and disease pressure. Pull: cool feed (18–22°C), increase aeration, reduce saturation.
pH drop locks out Ca/Mg/K and stresses roots. Pull: raise feed pH gradually and add leach to reset.
pH rise locks out Fe/Mn/Mg → chlorosis. Pull: lower feed pH, fix alkalinity (RO/acid), recheck runoff trend.
Root-zone EC climbs daily → burn and stalled uptake. Pull: lower feed EC and raise runoff % until PWEC≈Bulk.
Too much runoff strips nutrients → pale growth. Pull: reduce leach % and restore EC to target.
Compromised roots can’t uptake → chronic droop. Pull: increase oxygen (dryback/aeration), sanitize, manage temps, consider biologicals.
Media stays wet → hypoxic roots and slow growth. Pull: lengthen intervals and hit proper dryback; improve drainage.
Runoff is too low → salts build. Pull: add 10–20% leach temporarily and shorten peak gaps.
Runoff is too high → media drains out and deficiencies drift. Pull: cut leach % and raise EC modestly.
Cation imbalance shows as edge burn/distortion/chlorosis. Pull: rebalance Ca:Mg, moderate K/PK spikes, stabilize pH.
High K blocks Mg → interveinal chlorosis + edge burn. Pull: lower K/EC, add modest Mg, reset PWEC.
High pH locks micros; feeding more won’t help. Pull: lower pH/alkalinity and recheck runoff after 1–2 irrigations.
Low pH locks Ca/Mg/PK and irritates roots. Pull: raise pH gradually and reduce ammonium bias.
Na + alkalinity drives toxicity and pH drift. Pull: RO blend, purge with leach, and manage alkalinity.
Media EC is falling too fast → starvation trajectory. Pull: raise feed EC and reduce excessive runoff.
EC is high and you aren’t flushing it → burn risk. Pull: increase runoff % and lower feed EC until converged.
Feed and media aren’t converging—schedule mismatch. Pull: adjust shot/interval/runoff to drive PWEC≈Bulk.

conditions.condition: Nitrogen limitation under high photon flux
conditions.message: High light drives N demand → uniform chlorosis incoming. Pull: increase N/EC slightly or reduce DLI until stable.
Peak demand outpaces irrigation → wilt and Ca issues. Pull: add mid‑peak pulses and shorten intervals.
Too much water too fast → runoff waste and hypoxia. Pull: reduce shot size, space events, improve drainage.
Undersized schedule causes big VWC swings → stress and salt spikes. Pull: increase frequency and/or shot size to stabilize VWC band.
Dry + hot without irrigation is an emergency. Pull: irrigate now and lower VPD/leaf temp.
Cadence keeps media saturated → oxygen debt. Pull: lengthen intervals and hit dryback targets.
Low runoff lets salts accumulate. Pull: raise runoff % (10–20%) and lower feed EC until reset.
Too much runoff strips nutrients and destabilizes pH. Pull: reduce runoff % and restore EC.
Skipping peak-window irrigations causes daily crashes. Pull: schedule pulses during peak light/VPD and add alarms.
Too little water with long gaps → deep dryback and salt stacking. Pull: shorten gaps and increase shot volume.
Overwatering with bypass drain → hypoxia + bad data. Pull: shrink shot size and keep frequency.
VWC stays flat because shots are drowning the media. Pull: reduce shot size and allow real dryback.
VWC stays low because shots don’t wet fully. Pull: increase shot size to full saturation and confirm uniform wetting.
No runoff for days = hidden salt stack. Pull: rebuild schedule with periodic runoff; verify PWEC.
Different zones transpire differently; one size won’t fit all. Pull: improve uniformity and zone irrigation/emitters if needed.
Dehydration stress is maxed → burn risk. Pull: irrigate now, lower VPD, cool leaves.
Heat + long gaps causes repeat wilt cycles. Pull: add mid‑peak pulses and increase shot/shorten intervals.
Strong feed + not enough water burns roots fast. Pull: lower EC 10–20% and increase frequency/runoff to reset.
VPD stress plus salt stacking = edge burn recipe. Pull: lower VPD and leach to bring PWEC≈Bulk.
Rapid dehydration → wilt and scorch. Pull: drop leaf temp, raise RH, and irrigate immediately.
Strong feed + inadequate cadence burns roots fast. Pull: lower EC and increase frequency/runoff until stable.
Long gaps and tiny shots create dry pockets and spikes. Pull: shorten gaps and increase shot size.
Hot canopy drives photoinhibition and dehydration. Pull: reduce radiant load (raise/dim), improve cooling and airflow, keep VPD mid‑band.
Cold canopy stalls metabolism and uptake. Pull: raise air/leaf temps 2–3°C and avoid big night drops.
Air is too dry → stomata close and edges burn. Pull: humidify or lower temp to bring VPD into band.
Air is too wet → slow uptake and disease risk. Pull: dehumidify, raise VPD, and increase airflow (especially lights‑off).
Droop is oxygen debt, not thirst; more water makes it worse. Pull: increase dryback, improve aeration, and let roots breathe before resuming normal cadence.
New growth distortion/tip burn from weak Ca delivery. Pull: stabilize VPD, add peak pulses, ensure adequate Ca and root oxygen.
Heat accelerates dehydration and damages tops. Pull: lower leaf temp, raise RH slightly (to drop VPD), and dim peaks if needed.
Acidic media locks out Ca/Mg/K and stresses roots. Pull: raise feed pH gradually and add leach; recheck after 1–2 irrigations.
Alkaline media locks out Fe/Mn/Mg → chlorosis. Pull: lower feed pH, fix alkalinity, and recheck runoff trend.
Overnight depletion is too aggressive → morning stress and EC spikes. Pull: shorten night interval/shot, lower night VPD, consider pre‑lights‑off pulse.
Media stays too wet overnight → hypoxia risk and slow renewal. Pull: lengthen night interval or reduce late-day irrigation to hit dryback target.
